title: Fix profile display names and avatars to fully persist across sessions

requirements.text: Ensure user profile display names are fully persisted in stable backend storage so they survive canister upgrades and are consistently returned for the authenticated principal across all sessions.

quotes: Profile display names and avatars may not be fully persisted yet

acceptanceCriteria: A user who sets a display name and logs out, then logs back in, sees the same display name without re-entering it.
Display names survive canister upgrades via stable storage.
The getUserProfile backend call returns the saved displayName for any principal that previously called saveUserProfile.

requirements.text: Ensure user profile pictures (avatars) are fully persisted in stable backend storage so they survive canister upgrades and are consistently returned for the authenticated principal across all sessions.

quotes: Profile display names and avatars may not be fully persisting

acceptanceCriteria: A user who uploads a profile picture and logs out, then logs back in, sees the same avatar without re-uploading.
Profile picture data survives canister upgrades via stable storage.
The getUserProfile backend call returns the saved profilePicture blob/URL for any principal that previously saved one.

requirements.text: Update the frontend profile hooks (useUserProfile) and ProfileEditorDialog to correctly read and write display name and avatar from/to the backend, ensuring the UI reflects the persisted values immediately after login and after saving changes.

quotes: Profile display names and avatars may not be fully persisting

acceptanceCriteria: After saving a display name or avatar in ProfileEditorDialog, the new values are immediately visible in the sidebar and message feed without a hard refresh.
On login, the profile data fetched from the backend populates the display name and avatar in the UI.
MessageFeed shows sender avatars correctly for users who have set a profile picture.

requirements.text: Run a migration (migration.mo) if the existing userProfiles stable map uses a schema that does not include displayName or profilePicture fields, converting any legacy entries to the new schema so no profile data is lost on upgrade.

quotes: Profile display names and avatars may not be fully persisting

acceptanceCriteria: Canister upgrade does not drop or reset any previously stored user profiles.
Legacy profile entries with a `name` field are migrated to use `displayName`, preserving the value.
The migration is idempotent — running it twice produces the same result.

constraints: Do not change the authentication mechanism — Internet Identity remains the only auth provider.
All backend logic must remain in a single Motoko actor (backend/main.mo).
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
nonGoals: Adding new profile fields beyond displayName, profilePicture, and presence status.
Changing the Teams-inspired UI layout or navigation structure.
Implementing any new messaging, channel, or DM features.
Adding email or external storage for avatars.
